publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-01-27 23:26:31 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The collection includes original acetates from the 1960s to the early 1990s and paper and digital ephemera related to Deadly Dragon Sound System and to reggae history generally, including two hand painted signs advertising shows in Jamaica.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes original acetates from the 1960s to the early 1990s and paper and digital ephemera related to Deadly Dragon Sound System and to reggae history generally, including two hand painted signs advertising shows in Jamaica.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jeremy Freeman Reggae collection, #8700. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials separated from the resource: Panther's Collection by Mirjam Wirz (Rollo Press, undated); Sonidero City by Mirjam Wirz (undated); and Volcano Revisted: Kingston Dancehall Scene 1983 by Tero Kaski and Pekka Vuorinen (2011).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page. Acetate discs may not be removed from their sleeves.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: "A:" and "B:" indicate disc sides. Artists and track titles were transcribed from original handwritten labels wherever possible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous press materials, photographs, and an empty box from a Leroy Smart box set (Tuff Gong Records) .
